content.problemStatement:
Chef bought NNN items from a shop. Although it is hard to carry all these items in hand, so Chef has to buy some polybags to store these items.

111 polybag can contain at most 101010 items. What is the minimum number of polybags needed by Chef?


Input Format

The first line will contain an integer TTT - number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the number of items bought by Chef.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of polybags required.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of items Chef bought.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum number of polybags required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One item fits in a single polybag.
Exactly ten items fill one polybag.

solution.approach: Each polybag can hold at most 10 items. To minimise the number of bags we fill each bag as much as possible. The answer for a given N is ceil(N/10). Using integer arithmetic this can be computed as (N + 9) / 10. We repeat this for all test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
